blockingIssues.description: WCAG AA contrast failure: .result-recommendation.alternative uses #FF3300 background with #fff text at 12px bold. Contrast ratio 3.67:1; WCAG AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text (12px does not qualify as large text). Fix: darken to #CC2900 (~5.0:1) or use dark text on orange.

WCAG AA contrast failure: .tx-status.success uses #00AA00 text on actual #e8e8e8 background. Contrast ratio 2.54:1; fails even the 3:1 large-text threshold. Worker incorrectly calculated against white (4.57:1) but the actual background is #e8e8e8. Fix: darken to #007700 (4.7:1 on #e8e8e8) or change background to #fff.

appliedUpdates.description: Created color-system.md documenting the UI color palette, WCAG contrast ratios against actual backgrounds, known failures, and design rules. Prevents future workers from repeating the error of calculating contrast against white when the actual CSS background differs.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the Brutalist design rules color palette entry from 'black (#000) / white (#fff) + #0055FF accent' to list all four accent colors now in use: #0055FF (blue/primary), #CC2900 (orange/alternative — corrected from #FF3300 which fails contrast), #007700 (green/success — corrected from #00AA00), #CC0000 (red/error). This prevents workers from treating #0055FF as the only valid accent and from introducing ad hoc colors.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature add-color-accents (commit 0fea9f1) expanded the palette to four colors. Without documentation, future workers may add more colors or use the uncorrected hex values that fail WCAG AA.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Enforce that the lint check is mandatory in the verification step. Worker for add-color-accents ran typecheck and tests but skipped 'npm run lint'. The SKILL.md already lists lint as required, but the worker deviated without noting it. Consider making the lint step the first in the verification checklist to make skipping it more visible.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: add-color-accents handoff commandsRun lists only npm run typecheck, npm test, and curl health check. The fullstack-worker SKILL.md section 4 explicitly requires 'npm run lint'. Deviation was not flagged in the handoff skillFeedback.

rejectedObservations.observation: CSS text-truncation pattern (overflow:hidden; text-overflow:ellipsis; white-space:nowrap; min-width:0) is repeated in multiple places and could be documented as a utility class convention.
rejectedObservations.reason: The codebase uses inline CSS architecture in a single server.ts file — utility class conventions would require a style tag restructure. Low priority and inconsistent with the existing architecture pattern. Not actionable without broader refactoring.
